findings.summary: statistics.total_components reports 7 (unique refs) instead of 105 actual component instances in unannotated schematic
findings.details: This KiCad 5 legacy schematic is fully unannotated — all component references use '?' suffix (C?, R?, U?, etc.). The analyzer counts unique reference designators (7 unique refs) rather than actual component instances (105 entries in the components array, matching the 125 $Comp blocks minus power symbols). The BOM similarly collapses 10 capacitors to quantity=1 under 'C?', 19 resistors to quantity=1 under 'R?', and 72 ICs (68 keyboard switches + 3 LED drivers + 1 MCU) to quantity=1 under 'U?'. The statistics should count total instances, not unique ref strings.

findings.summary: 68 keyboard switches (keyboard:MX-RGB) classified as type 'ic' instead of 'switch'
findings.details: The schematic contains 68 mechanical keyboard switch symbols from the 'keyboard:MX-RGB' library. All 68 are classified as type 'ic' in the components array, causing the statistics to report 72 ICs (68 keyboard switches + 3 IS31FL3731 LED drivers + 1 AT90USB1286 MCU). The single explicit SW_Push switch (SW?) is correctly classified as 'switch'. The key_matrices detector finds 0 key matrices, missing the 68-key matrix. The misclassification is caused by the 'keyboard:MX-RGB' lib_id not being recognized as a switch type — the classifier likely falls back to 'ic' for unrecognized library symbols with the 'U' reference prefix.
